resources.string: Accessibility service for simulating touch events on the secondary screen
Cursor is not moved, perform single finger gestures

Touch in this area:
- Single finger swipe - %s
- Single finger tap - click
- Multi-finger gestures - perform multi-finger gestures
- Back button - press back on the target screen
- Home button - open the selected application again
- Moon button - black display to save power
- If a Bluetooth controller cannot control the display, try using the moon button to turn off the main screen, which forces the cursor to the secondary display

This app uses Sunshine (https://github.com/LizardByte/Sunshine).

This app uses DisplayLink® driver to support DisplayLink® devices. The driver is only used to add compatibility with DisplayLink® devices and contains no modifications.

- Users should comply with the relevant licensing terms of Synaptics when using DisplayLink® related features
- This app has no official association with Synaptics.
Exporting log files requires Shizuku permission

This only controls the output resolution of the DisplayLink chip in the docking station. For USB-C DisplayPort connections, the resolution is determined by Android automatically. For Moonlight wireless projection, resolution and bitrate can be changed in Moonlight\'s settings.
The auto-start client will show its IP
Single-app projection (can project launcher apps like Microsoft Launcher)
Automatically move mouse to external display (mouse only works on Android 15 with Shizuku; Bluetooth gamepads and keyboards follow system focus \u2014 use the screen off button to transfer focus)
Automatically move keyboard to external display
Enable main screen touch control for extended display
Auto scale to remove black bars
Auto screen off (requires using volume keys to wake up)
Use black image to simulate screen off
Automatically adjust main screen aspect ratio to match external display
Show floating back button in mirror mode
Prevent auto-lock (modifies the system screen timeout setting)
Disable REMOTE_SUBMIX to fix audio issues in Moonlight
Auto-hide floating back button (single tap to go back; double tap to return to projected app)
Auto-connect to Moonlight autostart edition (not compatible with standard Moonlight)
- Note: Android\'s built-in wireless display can be used with Shizuku for single-app projection.
- Note: USB-C DisplayPort displays do not require Shizuku for single-app projection.
Changes take effect on next app launch
Select a launcher app such as Microsoft Launcher for the best experience
Projection permission not granted. Tap Exit to close.

Projecting. Please disable battery optimization for this app in system settings, and lock the task in the recent apps list to prevent it from being killed. Touch may not work without Shizuku permission.
Please connect a display. Devices with USB 2.0 should use Moonlight wireless projection or a DisplayLink\u00ae compatible dock.
Failed to start user service to get logs
Check if the \'Mirror.log\' file has been exported in the download directory
Due to Android version limitiation, you need to tap the \'Simulate screen off\' button to use the touchscreen
